question_text: A chocolate bar contains 2000 kJ of chemical energy. Explain why the chemical energy stored in the chocolate cannot all be converted into kinetic energy if a person eats it and then runs.
correct_answer: The human body is not 100% efficient. Some energy is lost as heat, used for maintaining body functions, and wasted during digestion, so not all the chemical energy can be converted into the kinetic energy of running.
explanation: This tests understanding that energy transfers are never 100% efficient. The body loses energy through heat production, respiration, digestion, and maintaining bodily functions. Only a fraction of food energy becomes kinetic energy for movement.
